judge.rationale: This implementation is severe architectural slop. It does not implement SlidingWindowLimiter at all — there is no class, no constructor, no allow() method, no snapshot() method, and no actual rate-limiting logic. Instead, it attempts to import a nonexistent 'module_' (which will raise ImportError at runtime), then applies a bizarre meta-programming shim that tries to alias Dafny-compiled symbols. The code is entirely non-functional, opaque, indirect, and violates every Zen of Python principle: it is implicit rather than explicit, deeply nested in indirection, unreadable, and provides zero local auditability of the actual task semantics. The 'Dafny formal verification' framing in the oracle was clearly misread as an instruction to wrap a Dafny-compiled module rather than to write clean, verifiable Python code.
